steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the direction that Polynesian consonant inventories tend toward. The sentence then gives two data points — Sikaiana has 9 consonants, ʻŌlelo Hawaiʻi has 8 — compared with a global median of over 22. The word must capture what is distinctive about those low counts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — minimal: "Minimal" means as small as possible or very low in quantity. Sikaiana's 9 and Hawaiian's 8 consonants are far below the global median of 22+, so "toward the minimal" accurately names the pattern the data illustrates. ✓
  • Choice B — static: "Static" means unchanging over time. The sentence compares consonant counts across different languages at a single point, not how those counts change — so "static" is irrelevant to the contrast being drawn. ✗
  • Choice C — replicable: "Replicable" means able to be reproduced. Nothing in the passage suggests these inventories are being copied or replicated elsewhere; the passage is about size, not reproducibility. ✗
  • Choice D — melodic: "Melodic" describes a pleasing or musical sound quality. The passage discusses the number of consonants, not their acoustic character, so "melodic" is a category error. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
